steps_raw.instruction: Add 400 µL of Buffer B3 , gently invert the tube until neutralized, and incubate at room temperature for 00:02:00 . The color should be uniformly yellow when neutralized.

Add 200 µL of Buffer B2 , gently invert the tube 10 times (do not vortex), and incubate at room temperature for 00:01:00 . Color should change to dark pink, and solution will become transparent and viscous. Avoid incubating longer than 1 minute to prevent irreversible plasmid denaturation
Resuspend the pellet in 200 µL of Buffer B1 . Gently pipette to mix. Transfer to a pre-labeled 1.5 mL eppendorf tube.
Isolating and Resuspending Culture

Obtain a clear round-bottom 96w plate. Download the attached excel sheet. Record the specific plate map that will be used for each run on the excel sheet. OD600_analysis.xlsx The plate map below is an example for 2 samples. BLANK BLANK SAMPLE 1 SAMPLE 1 SAMPLE 2 SAMPLE 2
OD600 Measurement of Overnight Cultures
Preparation
Save and file the excel sheet in the appropriate location.

Wipe benches and pipettes with DNA-Away and then RNAse-Away. Afterwards, wipe with 70% ethanol. Light the bunsen burner & work closely to it while performing the Validation of Overnight Culture steps.

In the clear round-bottom 96w plate, add 100 µL of the appropriate media in duplicate in the designated wells from the plate map above. These are the BLANKS. The blanks are used to account for the background absorbance due to the media in which the cells are grown.
If the cultures are sufficiently grown (OD corrected > 1), proceed. Extinguish the bunsen burner.
Once finished reading, add 10% bleach to each sample well of the 96w plate. After 10 minutes, empty the plate down the drain with water. Discard the plate in a biohazard box.
Lysis & Neutralization
Discard the supernatant into a 10% bleach solution.
Nanodrop
Refer to the NanoDrop Protocol .
Waste Disposal
Empty benchtop sharps container.
Empty ethanol beaker into the ethanol waste collection site.
DNA Binding & Wash

Centrifuge lysate at 16000 x g, °C, 5 minutes . Pellet should be compact and adhered to the side of the tube. If it is not strongly adhered to the side of the tube, spin again at 16000 x g, °C, 5 minutes . Pellet should be compact; if it is not strongly adhered to the side of the tube.

Carefully transfer 800 µL supernatant to the spin column and centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Tilt the eppendorf tube away from the pellet and carefully pipette up the supernatant, ensuring the pipette tip does not touch the pellet or any debris stuck to the side of the tube.
Re-insert the column into the same collection tube and add 200 µL of Buffer BZ. Centrifuge for for 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Add 400 µL of Buffer WZ and centrifuge for 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Transfer column to a clean, pre-labeled 1.5 mL eppendorf tube. Use care to ensure that the tip of the column does not come into contact with the flow-through in the collection tube.
With the snap-cap open, let ethanol evaporate for 00:02:00 from the column while sitting in the 1.5mL eppendorf tube.
Elution
Add 45 µL pre-heated to 50 °C . Elution Buffer or NFW to the center of the column's matrix. Incubate for 00:05:00 at 37 °C . Spin for 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute .

Transfer the flow-through now in the 1.5mL eppendorf tube back into the column's matrix to maximize yield. Incubate for 00:03:00 at 37 °C . Spin for 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Although this step may decrease final elute volume, it can increase yield.
For short-term storage ( < 1 day), store at 4 °C . For long-term storage ( > 1 day), store at -20 °C .

In the clear round-bottom 96w plate, add 90 µL of appropriate media and 10 µL of each overnight culture (pipette to mix before transferring culture) in duplicate into the designated wells, to make 1:10 dilutions. Ensure to mix the wells thoroughly once added. Save overnight cultures for the following steps.
Begin pre-heating Elution Buffer or NFW to 50 °C in a bead-bath or incubator for the elution step later on.
Discard flow-through in the collection tube into the waste beaker labeled "Ethanol waste." Dab the bottom of the collection tube on a KimWipe to absorb any excess flow-through.
Add 10% bleach solution to remaining overnight cultures. Wait 00:30:00 . Pour down the drain with water. Dispose of culture tubes in biohazard.
Place the filter back into the same collection tube. Spin for 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Discard flow-through in the collection tube into a waste beaker labeled "Ethanol waste." Dab the bottom of the collection tube on a KimWipe to absorb any excess flow-through.

Read OD600 using the BioTek Synergy H1 microplate reader. Copy the blanks and the OD values into the excel sheet downloaded in a previous step. The sheet will perform the following calculations to correct the sample measurements. OD corrected = (OD sample − average (OD blanks ) ) * 10
